refusedRoutes.reason: Health cost planning page is safer after disclaimers, but hospital prices and insurance outcomes need human/domain review before registry exposure.
Nutrition education page is discoverable with disclaimers, but diet and medical-adjacent claims require domain review before registry exposure.
Education planning page avoids official GPA/admission claims, but should remain outside registry until academic-record wording is reviewed.
Cycle estimate page remains medical-adjacent and should not be promoted without clinician-domain review.
HR/legal-adjacent page is safer after rewrite, but employment outcome and contract wording should stay outside registry until specialist review.
Pregnancy date page is educational only and needs clinician-domain review before registry exposure.
Retirement planning page avoids guarantees, but finance and pension assumptions need specialist review before registry exposure.
Health education page should not be promoted until diagnosis-risk wording is reviewed by a domain specialist.
Religious calendar wording should remain conservative and not be promoted without religious/domain review.
Compliance calendar can imply official filing certainty and requires legal/admin review.
Bond/surety calculations can imply legal or financial certainty and require specialist review.
Religious giving guidance should not be registry-promoted without qualified review.
Cultural and family-negotiation content should stay bridge-only until cultural/legal review.
Zakat page is safer after rewrite, but it still needs religious review before promotion.
Partnership agreement draft remains legal-adjacent and should not be promoted without lawyer review.
Sickle-cell guidance remains medical/genetic-adjacent and needs qualified review.
Government allowance content can drift and should be verified against official sources before promotion.
Government allowance content can drift and should be verified against official sources before promotion.
Tenant screening is legal/privacy-adjacent and should not be promoted without jurisdictional review.
Halal review can imply certification and requires qualified religious/certification review.
Child growth content remains medical-adjacent and requires clinician-domain review.
Faraid inheritance content is religious and legal-adjacent and must remain outside registry until qualified review.

decision: No specialist queue page is safe enough for registry promotion in this gate. Keep all 22 routes discoverable only through existing page/link paths until specialist review clears them.
